meta.about_title: About Us — Zhonghai Synergy Group Limited (ZSG)
meta.contact_title: Contact Us — Zhonghai Synergy Group Limited (ZSG)

home.hero_title: Aggregating Global Green Energy, Leading the Carbon-Negative Future of Transportation
home.about_title: Leading the global renewable energy industry
home.about_body: ZSG is fundamentally reshaping the global energy landscape. We have strategically positioned ourselves at key global shipping hubs—including Sri Lanka, Panama, Egypt, and Indonesia—and taken the lead by investing $11.8 billion in Sri Lanka to develop the first "ZSG Green Hydrogen-Ammonia-Methanol Integrated Project". This initiative marks the world's first integrated base combining the production of green hydrogen, green ammonia, and green methanol. Supported by ancillary projects such as dedicated energy crop production bases and massive wind and solar power farms, we are building a vast "Carbon-Negative Mountain." This is not merely the creation of pioneering, closed-loop renewable energy infrastructure; it is a dedicated contribution toward achieving global "carbon neutrality" and "peak carbon" targets.
home.business_body: We innovatively adopt "Phase Change Migration-Driven Seawater Electrolysis" technology to directly produce high-purity hydrogen, achieving zero consumption of terrestrial freshwater, eliminating the need for reverse osmosis (RO) facilities, and avoiding concentrated brine discharge. This significantly protects marine animal habitats and coral reef ecosystems. In saline-alkali and drought-prone regions, we establish closed-loop biomass energy crop systems. By cultivating crops such as giant reed and employing the dual technology chain of "Circulating Fluidized Bed Gasification (CGAS)" and "Athermal Carrier Flash Pyrolysis," we maximize carbon emission reductions. We remain 100% export-oriented, meeting the green fuel bunkering demands of the global shipping market while indirectly boosting the chemical industry influence of the host countries.

about.page_subtitle: Synergizing Transnational Capital and Premier Scientific Matrices
about.section1_body: Zhonghai Synergy Group Limited (ZSG) is headquartered in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of China, dedicated to the research, development, and large-scale deployment of cutting-edge clean energy technologies, driving the global economy toward a low-carbon and sustainable future. The Group commands a world-leading technology ecosystem spanning green hydrogen, green methanol, and their derivatives, and serves as the initiating party and core operating entity of this project. Backed by substantial transnational capital investment, ZSG has established a strategic presence across major global shipping corridors, with projects spanning Sri Lanka, Panama, Egypt, and Indonesia, building a worldwide clean energy supply network that directly serves the international green marine fuel market.
about.section2_body: Top-tier EPC contractors include The Third Construction Engineering Company Ltd. of China Construction Second Engineering Bureau and China MCC5 Group Co., Ltd. — both Fortune 500 enterprises with full-industry-chain lifecycle management capacity. The financing syndicate platform, Zhonglian Construction International Holdings Co., Ltd., drives credit-line approvals through the Export-Import Bank of China, Sinosure, and HSBC.
about.section3_body: Our research and engineering network spans CRRC, the CAS Institute of Engineering Thermophysics, Tsinghua University, Tianjin University, Shenzhen University, Hainan University, Wuhan Lando Biotechnology Co., Ltd., Tianjin Hongri Technology Development Group Co., Ltd., Tianjin Saihong Environmental Engineering Co., Ltd., CAS Hefei Coal Gasification Technology Co., Ltd., and Kaifeng Deer Air Separation Industrial Co., Ltd.

business.page_subtitle: Dual-Core Technology, Flagship Projects, and Carbon-Negative Commitments
business.area1_body: ZSG eliminates conventional desalination barriers through interfacial phase-change on-site self-absorption purification, using pressure differentials across a hydrophobic gas-phase membrane. Water vapor is absorbed inline into standard alkaline electrolyzers, avoiding anode chlorine evolution and cathode scaling by rejecting impurity ions from raw saltwater (rejection rate >99.99%). With DC power usage of only 4.5 kWh/Nm³, the technology sets a new standard for electrolysis — requiring no freshwater and leaving no environmental impact.
business.area2_body: Standardized marginal-land cultivation of polyploid Giant Reed yields 75–105 metric tons of bone-dry matter per hectare, with over 86% structural carbohydrates, a heating value of 3,900–4,400 kcal/kg, 46.63% carbon content, negligible sulfur, and under 4% ash. Two thermochemical pathways follow: Pathway A uses the CAS CGAS atmospheric circulating fluidized bed gasification process (98–99% carbon conversion, 75–80% cold-gas efficiency) to feed green methanol loops; Pathway B uses Wuhan Landuo's no-heat-carrier regenerative radiant-tube flash pyrolysis (≥900°C within 6–9 seconds), producing green hydrogen at roughly one-third the cost of traditional water electrolysis, alongside premium biochar.
business.area3_body: ZSG rehabilitates barren soils through integrated biological, chemical, and automated-machinery techniques. Proprietary TRX-SA-01 carbon-based probiotic soil conditioners and TRX-SA-02 bio-based salt-leaching agents reduce soil salinity from 15‰ to below 3.98‰ within months, with no adverse chemical consequences. Soil porosity improves 18.7–21.6% while organic matter doubles. Municipal sewage-sludge aerobic fermentation lines (400 tons/day) and localized fertigation upcycle organic waste and promote energy-crop growth — a sustainable loop that transforms deserts into oasis.
business.area4_title: Flagship Project — Sri Lanka Integrated Complex
business.area4_body: The ZSG Green Hydrogen-Ammonia-Methanol Integrated Project (total investment USD 11.8 billion) is formally invited and fully backed by the Ministry of Energy of Sri Lanka. Positioned across the Southern (Hambantota) and Northeastern (Trincomalee) coastal regions, it features 4.5 GW of wind farms across three 1.5 GW phases; a phased 100,000-hectare Giant Reed plantation base; a dedicated chemical-grade port terminal with storage and international bunkering infrastructure for the 1.4 million t/a green product matrix; and the ZSG Comprehensive Headquarters in Colombo with digitalized staff living complexes. Construction is scheduled to commence in 2026, with 100% export orientation targeting global green shipping-fuel markets.
business.area5_body: Beyond Sri Lanka, ZSG is developing a portfolio of international assets: the Panama Port of Balboa Green Methanol Project, where ZSG acts as developer and full owner of a planned 1.4 million tons/year green methanol base serving global maritime lines; the China Tianjin Saihong Biomass Poly-generation Project, a 100,000 tons/year biomass-to-green-methanol facility; and the China Zhanjiang CIMC Enric Project, a collaborative 50,000 tons/year green fuel refinery.
business.area6_body: Giant Reed fixes 45–50% pure carbon per dry ton and sequesters over 50 tons of CO₂ per hectare annually — five times that of tropical forests. Across its lifecycle, every ton of dry biomass can deliver cumulative reductions of up to 3.04 tons of CO₂-equivalent, from sludge upcycling and soil carbon-sink enhancement to coal replacement and green methanol conversion. The complex is guaranteed to consume zero fossil fuel and is anchored on premier international certifications (ISCC Plus, EU RFNBO, the Equator Principles, IFC Performance Standards, ISO 14001). It directly creates tens of thousands of local jobs, trains local talent through a dedicated 'ZSG Training Academy' in partnership with CAS and Tsinghua University, and operates under 'Safety-First and Continuity-of-Service' principles to empower host governments to exceed their NDC commitments.

contact.page_subtitle: Embracing a New Era of Global Green Hydrogen Value Chains. ZSG welcomes long-term concessions, commercial off-take agreements, and strategic partnerships with global maritime lines, chemical buyers, and ESG financing syndicates.
contact.form_success: Thank you for your message. We'll get back to you soon.
